rm:LinkType.dcterms:description: ANA<->EXI
rm:LinkType.dcterms:title: Analysis
rm:LinkType.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a

rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: Main subject of requirement verification.
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: Verification Topic
QDate
rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: TRC attribute for requirements quality assessment.

rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: Verification activities can be performed at different levels : system, subsystems, upper system
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: Verification Level

rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: Requirement verification methods.
rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: Verification Methods

rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: Used to validate that the requirement is well traced by the downstream strata (inbound link on the requirement)
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: Flow Down Status

rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: Verification Strategy
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: For each requirement, the verification strategy will determine the most relevant level of action to perform.

rm:LinkType.dcterms:description: REQ<->REQ, REQ<->NEED
rm:LinkType.dcterms:title: Satisfaction
rm:LinkType.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a

rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: Safety and/or upper system acceptance of derived requirements.
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: Derived Content Impact Analysis
Validation Results Comment
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: Free text providing substantiation information on the validation result. Such information is provided when the validation result needs complementary justification according to evidences provided.
rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a

rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: External reference to a document or requirements for which Safran is not responsible (customer reference, etc.)
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: External Reference
rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a

rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: TRC attribute for requirements quality assessment.
rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: QNumber

rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: Evaluate supposed impact of the requirement on the Upper-System’s needs and safety aspects (IMPACT SUSPECTED / NO IMPACT TO BE CONFIRMED). Update the status with upper level system to confirm impact analysis (NO IMPACT OR IMPACT ACCEPTED / IMPACT NOT ACCEPTED).
rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: Derived Content Impact Status

rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: Design Parameters of the design which structure architecture system. As long as they are in an authorized range, the design can retain the same architecture hypothesis but beyond a threshold value, the design and architecture is questioned in a major way. Fixed by needs or requirements at the moment considered, prescribed by the environment of the perimeter considered, they allow to raise constraints to the higher system.
rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: Key Design Driver
DAL Level
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: Design Assurance Level
rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a

rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: TRC attribute for requirements quality assessment.
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: QSummary
rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a

rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: Resulting status of the validation activities. It is the summary status of all the validation steps associated to each method and evidence. This is the result of the Engineering review of the validation artefacts as required by the the validation plan. Passed: When all validation activities are achieved and all validation steps results are considered as “Passed” and then concluding that the requirement/document is valid (correct, complete and consistent). Failed : When all validation activities are achieved and at least one of the validation steps result is considered as “Failed” and then concluding that the requirement/document is not valid (correct, complete and consistent).
rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: Validation Status
Verification Coverage Status
rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: Coverage status of the requirement by its test cases and their associated test procedures
Check list used to assess the requirement validation.
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: Validation Checks
rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a

rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: Configuration Item Name
rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: N/A

rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: Sets of requirements to be managed
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: Batch

rm:LinkType.dcterms:description: Artifact and Module Reference
rm:LinkType.dcterms:title: BCI Reference
rm:LinkType.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a

rm:LinkType.dcterms:description: Artifact and Design Parameter
rm:LinkType.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
rm:LinkType.dcterms:title: KDD Reference

rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: Document Signatories

rm:defaultValue.rdf:value: Compiled by: Function or company|First name LAST NAME === Checked by: Function or company|First name LAST NAME === Approved by: Function or company|First name LAST NAME === Authorized by: Function or company|First name LAST NAME ===

rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: List of signatories of the document as per the following model : Compiled by: Function or company|First name LAST NAME === Checked by: Function or company|First name LAST NAME === Approved by: Function or company|First name LAST NAME === Authorized by: Function or company|First name LAST NAME ===

Explanatory wording or reference to the validation substantiation records or reports providing evidence of the correctness, completeness and consistency of the document/requirement to be validated. Validation evidence or artefact shall be provided according to the validation strategy. Meaning that the evidence of each activity conducted in front of each validation method shall be provided as well as the associated result.
rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: Validation Result
Version
rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: Revision Number incremented when the artifact has formally changed and been released (can be associated to a letter or digit for intermediate states between two releases).
TRC attribute for requirements quality assessment.
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: QLevel
rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a

rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: Verification Means
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: Verification means (benches, test equipment, models, etc.) are provided for verification activities. They have to be as representative of upper level system installation as possible regarding the tests objectives on the mean (performances, environment, etc).
rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: UID Prefix
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: Prefix for requirements identifier.

Additional content resulting from design or implementation decisions during the development process which are not directly traceable to higher-level requirements from stakeholder accountable for system safety.
Requirements with derived content may have Upper level system and/or safety impacts. Additional content resulting from design or implementation decisions during the development process which are not directly traceable to higher-level requirements from stakeholder accountable for system safety.
rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: Derived Content
Validation Methods
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: Requirement validation methods
rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: System or physical equipment to which the requirement is allocated
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: Allocation

rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description:
Explanatory text to describe rationale of the requirement.
- Refinement process leading to the current requirement from the upper requirement in particular provides the context of the requirement. - Justification of the design choices (extended / derived requirements) - Description or Justification of calculations leading to the current requirement
Rationale can be documented/completed through a reference to existing artefacts (design artefacts, assumption statements, lessons learnt, validation reports, etc.) Rationale are mandatory in case the requirement is not traceable (type = Derived /Extended) or if its traceability is complex (trace to more than one upper level requirements). Rationale is not required for “Direct Flow Down” requirements.
rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: Rationale

rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: Upward traceability kind : Direct Flow Down: When requirement is transferred from the upper level without any modification. Refined : Requirement that results from refinement of a need expressed at upper-level due to - Modification: requirement transferred with slight changes often due to the context of the specification or design layer. - Apportionment: requirement decomposition due to distribution of functional or performance contribution to architecture elements (functions, components). - Calculation : requirement is the result calculation of upper requirements (upper constraints fusion, units adaptation, …) Derived: New behaviour introduced resulting from design or implementation decisions and not traceable to upper level. Derived requirements shall be substantiated and submitted to System/Safety analysis to confirm the new behaviour has no safety impact. Extended: when at least a part of the requirement is considered as introducing new behaviour and cannot be traced to an upper-level requirement. Extended requirements shall be submitted to System/Safety analysis to confirm the new behaviour has no safety impact.
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: Traceability Type
rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: Data Classification
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: Establishes the sensitivity of internal information according to its level of confidentiality (or criticality - see GRP-0076)

rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: KDD impact analysis on the upstream and downstream components.
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: Key Design Driver Impact

rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: To record Engineering Judgement on the requirement maturity. Low: recognized as a draft requirement still requiring a high level of convergence or recognised as being candidate for change. Medium: still some limited areas in negotiation or to be converged. High : recognized as a stable requirement in its actual definition
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: Engineering Judgment Maturity

rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: Requirement Class
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: To identify the requirement category. Requirement class is used, in particular, to preliminary assess the verification methods and verification means that could be used.
To identify requirements having a strong influence on cost, schedule, risk, functionality or performance.
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: Key
rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: Owner
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: Department/Team owning the artifact

rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: Comment
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: N/A

rm:LinkType.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
rm:LinkType.dcterms:description: REQ<->ANA
rm:LinkType.dcterms:title: Justification

rm:LinkType.dcterms:description: Link between statements and requirements
rm:LinkType.dcterms:title: Allocation
rm:LinkType.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a

rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: Explanatory wording or reference to the verification substantiation records or reports providing evidence of the satisfaction of the requirement by the implemented system. Verification evidence or artefact shall be provided according to the verification strategy. Meaning that the evidence of each activity conducted in front of each verification method shall be provided as well as the associated result.
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: Verification Result
Flight Test Clearance
rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: To identify whether the requirement needs to be verified for Flight Test clearance.

rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: Resulting status of the verification activities. It is the summary status of all the verification activities associated to each method and evidence. This is the result of the Engineering review of the verification artefacts as required by the the verification plan. Initial intention before the verification activities : PASSED EXPECTED / PASSED EXPECTED WITH RISK / FAILED EXPECTED And then updated after verification activities : PASSED / FAILED
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: Verification Status

rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: Part Number of the specified system.
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: PN Reference
rm:AttributeDefinition.dc:creator: https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
https://elm702/jts/users/s018642a
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:description: Safety-related requirement: Requirement that meets at least one of the following conditions: - It is flowed down from a safety need expressed in the PSSA (safety objectives (quantitative or qualitative), development assurance levels, independence requirements and protection mechanisms such as critical software monitors) - At least one of its upstream requirements/needs is identified as safety-related.
rm:AttributeDefinition.dcterms:title: Safety Related

rm:LinkType.dcterms:description: Captures the relationship between a Requirements Management artifact and a Change Management item that tracks the implementation of the requirement artifact (e.g., a Task in IBM Engineering Workflow Management). Links of this type appear as 'Tracks' in Change Management.
rm:LinkType.dc:creator: ADMIN
rm:LinkType.dcterms:title: Tracked By

rm:LinkType.dcterms:description: Captures part-whole relationships between Requirements Management artifacts. These types of links are typically used to represent artifact hierarchies.
rm:LinkType.dcterms:title: Decomposition
rm:LinkType.dc:creator: ADMIN
ADMIN
rm:LinkType.dcterms:title: Elaborates
rm:LinkType.dcterms:description: Expresses an elaboration relationship between entities, where the subject entity elaborates the object entity. For example, a requirement elaborates a model element.

Captures when the content of a Requirements Management artifact has been created from content of another Requirements Management artifact. This type of link is created when performing extraction-based operations, for example, the 'Save As New' operation for a Text artifact.
rm:LinkType.dcterms:title: Extraction
rm:LinkType.dc:creator: ADMIN

rm:LinkType.dcterms:description: Relates two Requirements Management Glossary Terms that have the same meaning.
rm:LinkType.dcterms:title: Synonym
rm:LinkType.dc:creator: ADMIN

rm:LinkType.dcterms:description: The model element satisfies the requirement (e.g. The use case satisfies a functional requirement).
rm:LinkType.dcterms:title: Satisfy Architecture Element
rm:LinkType.dc:creator: ADMIN

rm:LinkType.dcterms:description: Captures the relationship between a Requirements Management artifact and an Architecture Management item that represents a model of the requirement artifact (e.g., a UML Use Case in IBM Engineering Systems Design Rhapsody - Model Manager). Links of this type appear as 'Derives From' in Architecture Management.
rm:LinkType.dc:creator: ADMIN
rm:LinkType.dcterms:title: Derives Architecture Element

rm:LinkType.dcterms:description: Captures glossary-based relationships between part of a Requirements Management artifact (e.g., a word or phrase) and a Glossary Term. This type of link is created when performing Term-based operations for Text artifacts, for example, the 'Create New Term' operation.
rm:LinkType.dc:creator: ADMIN
rm:LinkType.dcterms:title: Artifact Term Reference

rm:LinkType.dcterms:description: Tracks a general relationship between Requirements Management artifacts.
rm:LinkType.dc:creator: ADMIN
rm:LinkType.dcterms:title: Link

rm:LinkType.dc:creator: ADMIN
rm:LinkType.dcterms:title: Implemented By
rm:LinkType.dcterms:description: Captures the relationship between a Requirements Management artifact and a Change Management item that describes the implementation of the requirement artifact (e.g., a Story in IBM Engineering Workflow Management). Links of this type appear as 'Implements' in Change Management.

rm:LinkType.dc:creator: ADMIN
rm:LinkType.dcterms:title: Validated By
rm:LinkType.dcterms:description: Captures the relationship between a Requirements Management artifact and a Quality Management item that validates the implementation of the requirement artifact (e.g. a Test Case in IBM Engineering Test Management). Links of this type appear as 'Validates' in Quality Management.
The target is a refinement of the source. (e.g. a use case scenario might be a refinement of a textual requirement that describes the interaction).
rm:LinkType.dcterms:title: Refines Architecture Element
rm:LinkType.dc:creator: ADMIN

rm:LinkType.dcterms:description: Expresses a specification relationship between entities, where the subject entity is specified by the object entity. For example, a requirement is specified by a model element.
rm:LinkType.dcterms:title: Specified By
rm:LinkType.dc:creator: ADMIN

rm:LinkType.dcterms:description: The model element has a trace to the requirement (e.g. an attribute or its value are traced to a requirement).
rm:LinkType.dc:creator: ADMIN
rm:LinkType.dcterms:title: Trace Architecture Element

rm:LinkType.dc:creator: ADMIN
rm:LinkType.dcterms:title: Affected By
rm:LinkType.dcterms:description: Captures the relationship between a Requirements Management artifact and a Change Management item that has an effect on the implementation of the requirement artifact (e.g., a Defect in IBM Engineering Workflow Management). Links of this type appear as 'Affects' in Change Management.
Expresses an elaboration relationship between entities, where the subject entity is elaborated by the object entity. For example, a requirement is elaborated by a model element.
rm:LinkType.dc:creator: ADMIN
rm:LinkType.dcterms:title: Elaborated By

rm:LinkType.dcterms:description: Tracks a containment relationship between Requirements Management artifacts. These types of relationships occur when performing operations such as 'Insert Artifact' and 'Insert Image' for a Text artifact.
rm:LinkType.dcterms:title: Embeds
rm:LinkType.dc:creator: ADMIN

rm:LinkType.dcterms:title: References
rm:LinkType.dc:creator: ADMIN
rm:LinkType.dcterms:description: Captures the relationship between a Requirements Management artifact and another Requirements Management artifact in a different RM instance or external project area.

rm:LinkType.dc:creator: ADMIN
rm:LinkType.dcterms:description: Expresses a specification relationship between entities, where the subject entity further clarifies or specifies the object entity. For example, a requirement specifies a model element.
rm:LinkType.dcterms:title: Specifies
